XONLINE_USER

Contains information about an online player.

typedef struct{
    XUID xuid;
    CHAR szGamertag[XONLINE_GAMERTAG_SIZE];
    DWORD dwUserOptions;
    BYTE passcode[XONLINE_PASSCODE_LENGTH];
    BYTE reserved[XONLINE_USER_RESERVED_SIZE];
    HRESULT hr;
} XONLINE_USER, *PXONLINE_USER;

Members

xuid
XUID structure that contains information about the player.
szGamertag

Null-terminated string that contains the player's gamertag. The format of the gamertag must adhere to the following rules:
  • Minimum of one character in length.
  • Maximum of 15 characters in length.
  • Can include only the following characters: A–Z, a–z, 0–9, and [space].
  • Cannot begin with a [space] character.
  • Cannot have multiple contiguous [space] characters.
  • Must begin with an alphabetic character (A–Z, a–z).
  • Cannot end with space.

The gamertag is case insensitive for comparisons, but case should be preserved when stored. For example, Ubba and UBBA are the same and hence cannot both exist as accounts in the system, but which ever one was entered into the system, the case is preserved.

dwUserOptions

Value that specifies player options. Can be a combination of the following values:
Value Description
XONLINE_USER_OPTION_CAME_FROM_MU Indicates the player account information came from an Xbox Memory Unit (MU).
XONLINE_USER_OPTION_REQUIRE_PASSCODE Indicates the player requires a pass code to log on.

For an account that came from an Xbox MU, you can use the following values to determine the port and slot associated with that player.

Value Description
XONLINE_USER_OPTION_MU_PORT_MASK Value to use with dwUserOptions to determine the port associated with the player.
XONLINE_USER_OPTION_MU_PORT_SHIFT Value to shift right after using the XONLINE_USER_OPTION_MU_PORT_MASK value.
XONLINE_USER_OPTION_MU_SLOT_MASK Value to use with dwUserOptions to determine the slot associated with the player.
XONLINE_USER_OPTION_MU_SLOT_SHIFT Value to shift right after using the XONLINE_USER_OPTION_MU_SLOT_MASK value.

passcode
Array of BYTE values that contain the pass code used to authorize access to this account. If the dwUserOptions member does not contain the XONLINE_USER_OPTION_REQUIRE_PASSCODE flag, this member is ignored.
reserved
Reserved. Do not use.
hr

HRESULT value that is used to report any warnings or errors specific to this player after sign-in.
Value Description
XONLINE_S_LOGON_USER_HAS_MESSAGE The sign-in succeeded, but the player has at least one unread Xbox Live message. The title must provide the option of booting into the Xbox Dashboard to allow the player to view the message. Use XLaunchNewImage with a reason code of XLD_LAUNCH_DASHBOARD_ACCOUNT_MANAGEMENT to boot to the Dashboard.
XONLINE_E_LOGON_USER_ACCOUNT_REQUIRES_MANAGEMENT The sign-in failed because of a problem with the player's account. The title must provide the option of booting into the Xbox Dashboard to allow the player to address the problem. Use XLaunchNewImage with a reason code of XLD_LAUNCH_DASHBOARD_ACCOUNT_MANAGEMENT to boot to the Dashboard.

Remarks

Use the XOnlineGetUsers function to obtain an array of XONLINE_USER structures that contain player information stored on the local Xbox console's hard disk or in memory units.

Use the XOnlineGetLogonUsers function to obtain an array of XONLINE_USER structures for players that are currently logged on.

Use the XOnlineAreUsersIdentical macro to determine if two different XONLINE_USER structures represent the same player.

Requirements

Header: Declared in XOnline.h.

See Also
